notes: Mak is a flexible tempo and economy hero. Two major win conditions: TEMPO (win fast on strong early board) or EXODIA (Bottled Explosion/Goop Flail scaling). Both share the same skill pool. Stabilize with strongest opener, use catalysts and reagents throughout, pivot into first high-ceiling line your board supports.

item_tier_list.description: Full Mak tier list from the Flex Mak Guide (Season 12). Items not listed here score as unranked (suboptimal in late). Covers all main build lines: Potion, Weapons, Poppy, Self-Poison, Satchel/Regen, Torch, Relic, Sands/Femur, Calc/Retort.

signals: Flying Potion plus Boiling Flask keeps Potion and Torch shells open.
Rapid Injection System plus Adrenal Converter means Self-Poison and Poppy boards stay live.
An early Satchel with strong econ is a reason to lean into Regen flex.
Calcinator or Retort plus enough generators by Day 3 is a real commit signal, not just value.
Poppy Field found at any point — transition from Self-Poison to Poppy when possible.
Femur of the Thumper found Day 6+ — pivot Sands/Femur for near-guaranteed 10-win.
Ethergy Conduit or Palanquin found with crit scaling — Relic line becomes viable.
Any build can reach S tier with sufficient charge/haste/multicast scaling — always look for Atmospheric Sampler, Library, Laboratory, Scales.

early.description: Tempo first, then econ. Use the strongest opener you see and keep flexible outs to potions, poison, weapons, or calc engines. Roll reagents and catalysts throughout the run for enchanted item highrolls.
early.notes: Best early build-arounds from the guide: Mortar & Pestle (best early pick, catalyst generator for all weapons lines), Refractor (flexible carry + scaling), Torch (wins early fights, great Day 1 quest), Idol of Decay (poison starter), Aludel (poison enabler), Flying Potion + Boiling Flask (hunt at all costs for Potion line). Calcinator and Retort are strong if board is already stable. Random lifesteal weapons are fine if Mortar & Pestle is online.

early_mid.description: Commit around the strongest shell you have while preserving realistic pivots into Mak's late-game payoff boards.
early_mid.notes: Guide emphasis: build around the carry you already found, buy charge and crit when they support your shell, save enough gold to convert Day 6 shops into a real end-game board. Check Expeditions on Day 6 and 9.

archetypes.name: Potion Setup
archetypes.notes: Hunt Flying Potion and Boiling Flask at all costs. Even reroll mediums with catalysts if needed. Potion Distillery is the premium support piece. Pivot into first strong end-board carry rather than tunneling on one payoff.

archetypes.name: Weapons Tempo
archetypes.notes: Mortar plus lifesteal and haste lets random weapons carry while you search for a real finisher. Save money on chocolates due to lifesteal sustain. Bronze base damage skills are very strong here.

archetypes.name: Poison Tempo
archetypes.notes: Strong early poison shells usually turn into Self-Poison or Poppy boards later. Keep Poppy Field as the upgrade target.

archetypes.name: Torch Tempo
archetypes.notes: Complete Torch purchase quest on Day 1 — consistent and very strong. Take enchanted small item if close to finishing quest. Fire Claw and Flying Potion now look like real bridges into the late Torch/Athanor burn shell. Great pivot item: if the burn line doesn't come together, pivot to potions easily.

archetypes.name: Satchel Setup
archetypes.notes: Only real if Satchel shows up before Day 6 and econ is healthy. Peacewrought is very strong here for refund value on each potion buy.

archetypes.name: Econ Engine
archetypes.notes: Needs enchanted Calc or Retort to be viable in current meta. When generator count is there by Day 3, this is a real commit line. Peacewrought helps with defense while engine comes online.

archetypes.name: Relic / Charge Setup
archetypes.notes: Focus on charge and haste effects for looping. Relic line needs crit to function. Laboratory charges all enchanted items and relics.

archetypes.name: Sands/Femur Setup
archetypes.notes: Early carries are Refractor, Raven, Fireflies (especially enchanted). Hunt Femur aggressively from Day 6+, even rolling large gold items. Finding it early is near-guaranteed 10-win.

late.description: Mak closes runs by converting a flexible tempo shell into a high-ceiling payoff board. Two main win conditions: TEMPO (win before opponent assembles perfect board) or EXODIA (Bottled Explosion/Goop Flail scaling). Both use the same skill pool so flex between them based on what you found.
late.notes: Any Mak build can reach S tier with sufficient charge/haste/multicast scaling. Always look for Atmospheric Sampler, Library, Laboratory, Scales, Pendulum as universal upgrades. Roll reagents and enchanted item rewards throughout the run.

archetypes.name: Potion Flex
archetypes.condition: Wants Flying Potion and Boiling Flask online. Hunt at all costs including rerolling medium items with catalysts.
archetypes.notes: End boards ranked: 1. Bottled Explosion (base damage is king — nodes, skills, loot; doubles with Boiling Flask). 2. Vat of Acid (go to Curio for cheap items/tags; check monster fights for tag items). 3. Athanor + Torch (burn pivot). 4. Plague Glaive. Distillery is premium support — transforms smalls into potions each shop visit. Recent Vial Launcher evidence supports Energy Potion as a useful accelerator. Focus on base damage scaling for Bottled Explosion via nodes, skills, and loot.

archetypes.name: Weapons Flex
archetypes.condition: Mortar & Pestle online. Two deciding factors: Improvised Weaponry skill available, or already winning every early fight on track for tempo 10-win.
archetypes.notes: Strong tempo line with multiple pivots. Mortar + haste and sustain lets random weapons bridge into real late-game carry. Improvised Weaponry skill is the key scaling option. Carpet line: save money since early board found immediately, but needs crit items to work in endgame.

archetypes.name: Poppy Flex
archetypes.condition: Needs Poppy Field. Transition into this from any Self-Poison board — you will already have most small weapons. Focus on upgrading Poppy base poison aggressively.
archetypes.notes: Meta keeps getting faster and this build can match that speed. Only one core item required. Easier to pivot into than other builds. Goal: one-shot opponent with crit + charge + poison scaling rather than prolonged fight. Upgrade Poppy Field base poison aggressively. Only large item with no vendor tags.

archetypes.name: Self-Poison Flex
archetypes.condition: Wants Rapid Injection System and Adrenal Converter (or close substitutes). Tempo-oriented — wins easier on early fights. If needing late-game win, pivot to Poppy instead.
archetypes.notes: Classic Mak poison engine. Sustain required — Infused Bracers next to Spider Mace for constant lifesteal is the most reliable method. Test Subject Alpha to diamond ASAP (double poison + 2s reduced CD). Plague Glaive needs targeted charge (Excavation Tools) or haste. Spider Mace + Bracers solve startup while you pivot to payoff.

archetypes.name: Satchel - Regen
archetypes.condition: Needs Satchel before Day 6 and healthy econ. Infinite Potion is the second core. Boiling Flask very strong if findable.
archetypes.notes: Peacewrought: play in PvE fights for extra Peacewrought value. Satchel combos well with Calc/Retort — both rely on potions for support. With enough permanent regen from Tome of Yyahan (and regen nodes based on level), you can skip Satchel entirely. Weakness: vulnerable to one-shots and high burst — need CC or invulnerability.

archetypes.name: Torch Flex
archetypes.condition: Torch online. Burn is doing better in current meta and transitions into potions nicely. Needs defense or CC for board.
archetypes.notes: Complete Torch purchase quest Day 1 — taking enchanted small item when close is recommended. Recent burn samples validate Eternal Torch plus Fire Claw, Athanor, Flying Potion, Torch, and Blazehowl Warden as the burn spine. Lib + Lab combo is a strong scaling option. Palanquin variant needs a defensive enchant. Transitions cleanly into Potion line once support items overlap.

archetypes.name: Relic Flex
archetypes.condition: Needs Laboratory or Ethergy Conduit as anchor. Focus on freeze/CC items for defense. Both Ethergy Conduit and Palanquin require crit to function.
archetypes.notes: Focus on charge and haste effects for looping. Laboratory charges all enchanted items and relics by 2s — great but awkward mid-game if board is insufficiently enchanted. Ethergy Conduit + Palanquin = Exodia combo. Blank Slate is suspicious (be selective). Do not click Life Conduit.

archetypes.name: Sands / Femur
archetypes.condition: Hunt Femur of the Thumper aggressively from Day 6+, even rolling large gold items. Early carries are Refractor, Raven, Fireflies (especially enchanted). Karnok is a tough matchup due to Chains.
archetypes.notes: Finding Femur early is near-guaranteed 10-win. Damage scaling skills open up many board options — fight Kyver Drone for these. Tempo Calc + Fireflies is a viable early variant. Without Femur, fall back to generic wincons like scaled Calcinator or Secret Formula.

archetypes.name: Burn
archetypes.condition: Eternal Torch as the primary burn engine with Fire Claw providing additional damage output.
archetypes.notes: Two-sample evidence from recent builds. Variant of the broader Torch Flex line focused on stacking Eternal Torch and Fire Claw together as the primary burn spine rather than relying on Torch as a solo engine. Treat as a narrower commitment within the Torch Flex direction.

archetypes.name: Athanor
archetypes.condition: Athanor as a primary value engine alongside Infinite Potion and Show Globe.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Athanor combined with Infinite Potion and Show Globe suggests a potion-triggered burn scaling loop. Treat as a work-in-progress until more evidence accumulates.

archetypes.name: Burn Poison
archetypes.condition: Calcinator and Retort with burn support layered on top via Library, Scales, and Strength Potion.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Variant of the Calc/Retort line that layers burn support (Athanor, Ice Claw) on top of the standard econ engine. Useful framing if the Calc/Retort board has burn carry items available but lacks the traditional Strength Potion combo.

archetypes.name: Cauldron
archetypes.condition: Cloud Wisp and Earrings as the trigger engine, supported by Atmospheric Sampler for charge scaling.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Cloud Wisp and Earrings form an unusual trigger shell. Atmospheric Sampler and Scales are the natural charge/scaling support. Treat cautiously until more samples confirm the identity.

archetypes.name: Frozen Flame
archetypes.condition: Frozen Horror as the primary carry, backed by Vial Launcher and Cellar for potion-fed sustain.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Frozen Horror is a B-tier weapon that pairs with potion and library support for a mid-range tempo board. Treat as exploratory until further evidence.

archetypes.name: Ice Claw
archetypes.condition: Ice Claw as the primary weapon, with Blank Slate providing flexible enchant scaling and Athanor for burn support.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Ice Claw is an A-tier weapon that benefits from Blank Slate's enchant flexibility. Athanor adds burn output. Likely a sub-variant of the Weapons Flex direction rather than a fully distinct line.

archetypes.name: Poison Freeze
archetypes.condition: Idol of Decay as the poison anchor with Atmospheric Sampler providing charge scaling.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Idol of Decay as the primary poison trigger with Atmospheric Sampler scaling. Crow's Nest provides charge density. Likely overlaps the Self-Poison Flex direction — treat as a variant with an Atmospheric Sampler focus.

archetypes.name: Potion
archetypes.condition: Flying Potion, Satchel, and Boiling Flask assembled together as a combined potion-regen shell.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Combines elements of Potion Flex and Satchel-Regen into a hybrid shell. Flying Potion + Satchel + Boiling Flask together form a dense trigger engine. Vial Launcher is the primary weapon payoff. Distinct from standard Potion Flex by having Satchel as a core piece.

archetypes.name: Vial Launcher
archetypes.condition: Vial Launcher as the primary weapon, supported by Crocodile Tears and Recycling Bin for ammo and board cycling.
archetypes.notes: Low-confidence single-sample archetype. Vial Launcher focused on ammo cycling via Crocodile Tears and Recycling Bin. Distinct from general Potion Flex in that Vial Launcher is the named win condition rather than a flex carry. Recent Potion Flex evidence supports Energy Potion as an accelerator alongside Vial Launcher.

archetypes.name: Calc / Retort
archetypes.condition: Wants enchanted Calcinator or Retort early — key to viability in current meta. Needs enough catalyst and reagent generators to justify full econ commit. Must commit early.
archetypes.notes: Strength Potion (silver small) gives all items 100% crit — must-buy, activates before Calc. Hourglass (bronze small) reduces adjacent CD so Strength Potion always fires before Retort. Insect Wing on Strength Potion ensures it fires before Retort. Distillery transforms reagent left each hour — must-buy, frees stash space for Tome. Laboratory charges all enchanted/relics by 2s (pairs with Black Pepper, Library, Scales, Wand). Library lowers CDs — better out of the box than Lab but lower cap. Peacewrought provides defense while engine scales. Pivot out aggressively if: no upgrade opportunities in a while, losing early rounds, or no Strength Potion found.
